text:
User: I need more logging whneever the daemon does something, when it routes to a session, when it starts a new agent, why it started it, when it creates a new ordinal, why it created it, etc, etc -- use 5 sonnet parallel agents to decide stuff like this to log -- ideally I'll be able to run `tenex-edge daemon` and see colorized output on stdout in addition to seeing the same ouput written to a daemon.log file [...] - `tenex-edge daemon` is now a visible subcommand (alias `__daemon` so the spawner still works) [...] **Task 1**: Add comprehensive logging to the `tenex-edge` daemon. When running `tenex-edge daemon`, produce colorized output on stdout AND write the same to daemon.log simultaneously. Log all key daemon operations: routing to sessions, starting new agents (with reasons), ordinal creation (with reasons), subscriptions, relay events. Use 5 parallel Sonnet agents to decide what to log in each subsystem. [...]      - `pub fn init_daemon_logging(log_path: &Path) -> Result<()>` — installs two layers when stdout is a terminal (colored stdout + plain file), single plain layer when detached [...]      - Changed `__daemon` hidden variant to visible `daemon` subcommand with alias:
     ```rust
     /// Start the per-machine daemon in the foreground...
     #[command(name = "daemon", alias = "__daemon")]
     Daemon,
     ```
     - The auto-spawner in `spawn.rs` still passes `"__daemon"` which resolves via alias [...]    - **Dual-output logging**: Solved with `std::io::IsTerminal::is_terminal()` — when foreground, two separate `layer()` instances (one ANSI stdout, one plain file with `Mutex<File>`); when detached, single plain stdout layer (the fd redirect already delivers to file).
   - **Rich colors without noise**: Used a custom `Fields` visitor to separate message from key-value pairs, then rendered each part with different owo-colors styling (bold white message, dim keys, bright_cyan values, level-colored background badges).
